doc:
Pure-TS port of MPFR's mpfr_dump (mpfr/src/dump.c L127-L131). One-line C body: `mpfr_fdump(stdout, x)`.

The TS port DELEGATES to the shipped mpfr_fdump (which returns the formatted string per its idiomatic-TS lift; see eval/functions/mpfr_fdump/spec.json). The C side writes to stdout; the TS side returns the string. Callers wanting stdout behavior do `console.log(mpfr_dump(x))` themselves.

Class 'misc' (1000ms per-case budget). Tag minimums per Rule 7 apply -- input domain is any well-formed MPFR.
divergence_from_c:
Two divergences (inherited from mpfr_fdump):

  1. Return shape: C void (writes to stdout) -> TS string (returns the formatted dump).

  2. Parameter list: C takes (mpfr_srcptr x); TS takes (x). The stream is implicit (stdout) in C and absent in TS.

The trailing newline is part of the returned string (matches mpfr_fdump's output format).
refs: mpfr/src/dump.c L127-L131 -- the C reference body (mpfr_dump = mpfr_fdump(stdout, x)).
eval/functions/mpfr_fdump/spec.json -- the canonical spec for the format details (mpfr_dump inherits).
